scenes.text:
If it takes less than two minutes,
do it right now. No excuses.

Procrastination feeds on momentum. The longer you wait, the harder it gets. But here is the hack. Almost every big task can be broken into a two-minute starting
point. Want to work out? Just put on your shoes. Want to write? Just open the document. Want to read? Just open the book to page one.

The hardest part of any task is the first two minutes. Once you start,
the momentum carries you. Your brain switches from resistance mode to flow mode.

You do not need to feel ready. You
just need to start. Starting is the strategy.

What have you been putting off? Do the
two-minute version right now. Follow for productivity hacks.
